subjects.name: Mathematics
subjects.description: Numbers, patterns, and problem solving that build confidence for school and exams.

subjects.name: Science
subjects.description: Everyday concepts, experiments, and clear explanations you can use in class and beyond.

topics.title: Number Basics
topics.summary: Get comfortable with numbers, operations, and reading a problem carefully.

topics.title: Patterns and Relationships
topics.summary: See how one quantity changes with another and spot useful connections.

topics.title: Organizing Problems
topics.summary: Learn how to break a problem into smaller steps and keep your work clear.

topics.title: Science Basics
topics.summary: Understand ideas, examples, and the reason behind each result.

topics.title: Energy and Change
topics.summary: Study how things transform as they move through a system.

topics.title: Control and Systems
topics.summary: Cover how one part of a system can guide another part.

examContexts.title: Mathematics study plan
examContexts.description: Exam-focused preparation for number sense, patterns, and structured problem solving.

examContexts.title: Science study plan
examContexts.description: Exam-focused preparation for concepts, examples, and application.

questions.prompt: Which operation helps you find the total of equal groups?
questions.explanation: Multiplication helps combine equal groups into one total.

questions.prompt: Name two ways to show a relationship between numbers.
questions.explanation: Tables and graphs make relationships easier to see.

questions.prompt: What problem does simplifying expressions help reduce?
questions.explanation: Simplifying reduces clutter and repeated work.

questions.prompt: Which example shows a change you can observe?
questions.explanation: Ice melting is an easy example of a visible change.

questions.prompt: Name two examples of energy you notice in daily life.
questions.explanation: Light, heat, sound, and motion are common examples of energy in daily life.
